input.description: Short conversation with intentional pauses to test VAD + window logic

output.polishedSample:
Agent: Karen, I appreciate you stopping by. So tell me, what's on your mind today? Well, I've been thinking a lot about the trip we have planned for next year. The Italy trip? Yes. And I'm worried about whether we can afford it after my husband retires in March. That's a fair question. Let me pull up your projections. OK, so based on our last review, you have about 220,000 in liquid savings. Plus the brokerage account of 350,000. The trip is going to cost about $15,000 in. Right. That sounds about right. Honestly, you can do this trip without it affecting your retirement plan at all. The $15,000 is less than 4% of your liquid savings, and you'll replenish it within a few years from your normal savings rate. Oh, that's a relief. I was stressed about it. Don't be. Go enjoy Italy. I will however suggest you wait until after the first quarter results come out in April before you book the more expensive flights, because we want to see if the year starts well. That makes sense, I'll let you know. And book travel insurance, at your age it's worth it.

Client: Thank you, Karen. I appreciate your guidance. I'll definitely consider those suggestions and plan accordingly.
output.summaryTLDR: Agent Karen reassured Client about funding an Italy trip post-retirement, emphasizing minimal impact on savings and suggesting to wait for first quarter results before booking expensive flights and to book travel insurance.
